metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an assertion in AC1's oracle that each coaching entry carries the correct `decision` field value (e.g., the blocking entry has `decision='block'` and the advisory entry has `decision='advisory'`), so the distinguishability requirement stated in the 'What' section is verifiably covered.
evidence: 'tagging each coaching note with the finding's decision so consumers can distinguish'
AC1 oracle: 'asserting coaching coverage over a synthetic verdict with 1 block + 1 advisory yields 2 coaching entries, each carrying guide_url' — no assertion on decision field
findings.finding: The 'What' section states coaching entries for blocking findings will be 'tagging each coaching note with the finding's decision so consumers can distinguish' — but no acceptance criterion verifies the decision/tag field is present on the coaching entry. AC1's oracle only checks for 2 entries and guide_url; it does not assert the decision field exists or has the correct value.
findings.location: What section (tagging requirement) vs. Acceptance Criteria AC1
scenarios: An implementation omits the decision tag entirely; all four ACs pass green, but consumers cannot distinguish blocking from advisory coaching entries at runtime — the stated purpose of the tagging is silently unmet.
findings.suggested_fix: Extend AC1 oracle to: '…yields 2 coaching entries; the entry for the blocking finding carries `decision="block"` and `guide_url`; the entry for the advisory finding carries `decision="advisory"` and `guide_url`.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `src/rebar/llm/workflow/gates/plan-review.yaml` to the edit-target list: update `coach_gate` branch condition and `coach_inputs` `surviving` binding to include blocking findings alongside surfaced advisories.
evidence: plan-review.yaml:188: `when: ${{ steps.decide.outputs.surfaced }}` — the coach_gate branch fires only on non-empty surfaced advisories
plan-review.yaml:180: `surviving: ${{ steps.decide.outputs.surfaced }}` — only surfaced advisories fed to coach_inputs
Plan says: 'Implementation seam: the pass-4 call in src/rebar/llm/plan_review/passes.py' — plan-review.yaml never mentioned
findings.finding: The plan omits `src/rebar/llm/workflow/gates/plan-review.yaml` as an edit target, even though it is the authoritative file gating Pass-4 coaching on advisory-only findings (`coach_gate` branch `when: ${{ steps.decide.outputs.surfaced }}`) and binding only `surfaced` as `surviving` to `coach_inputs`. Widening the finding set without updating the YAML leaves the branch condition and the `surviving` binding unchanged, so blocking findings will never reach the coach.
findings.location: What / 'Implementation seam' paragraph; src/rebar/llm/workflow/gates/plan-review.yaml lines 184-226 and 262-304
scenarios: A BLOCK verdict with 1 blocking + 0 advisory findings: the coach_gate branch `when: steps.decide.outputs.surfaced` is falsy, taking the else arm with `notes: []`, so blocking findings are coached with empty notes — the AC is not satisfied.
A BLOCK verdict with 1 blocking + 1 advisory: coach_inputs gets only the advisory as `surviving`; blocking finding is invisible to the coach.
findings.suggested_fix: In plan-review.yaml, change `surviving: ${{ steps.decide.outputs.surfaced }}` in `coach_inputs` to pass the union of blocking + surfaced (e.g. as a merged list or a new combined binding), and update the `coach_gate` branch `when:` condition to fire when either blocking or surfaced is non-empty.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `workflow_ops.py::plan_review_coach_inputs` to the edit-target list and widen its `surviving` assembly to include blocking findings passed via the workflow inputs.
evidence: workflow_ops.py:366: `surviving = list(ctx.inputs.get('surviving') or [])` — no blocking findings fetched
workflow_ops.py:374: `instructions = passes.coach_instructions(surviving, applicable)` — coach listing built from advisory-only surviving
Plan names only: 'passes.py (MOVE_REGISTRY lives there)', 'orchestrator.py:515-530'
findings.finding: The `plan_review_coach_inputs` workflow op in `workflow_ops.py` is an unenumerated edit target: it reads `surviving = list(ctx.inputs.get('surviving') or [])` and passes only that to the coach listing builder — blocking findings are not included. The plan names `passes.py` and `orchestrator.py` but not this op, which is the live-path consumer of `surviving`.
findings.location: What paragraph; src/rebar/llm/plan_review/workflow_ops.py:360-375
scenarios: Even if the YAML is updated to pass blocking findings, `plan_review_coach_inputs` ignores them — the coach prompt instructions list only advisories, and the blocking finding gets no coaching note.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `workflow_ops.py::plan_review_coach` to the edit-target list and update its trigger computation to union blocking findings' criteria alongside surfaced advisories'.
evidence: workflow_ops.py:497: `surviving = list(ctx.inputs.get('surfaced') or [])` — trigger computation uses only surfaced advisories
workflow_ops.py:498: `triggers = {c for f in surviving for c in f.get('criteria', []) or []}` — blocking findings' criteria excluded from trigger set
Plan's scope says 'widen the finding set it receives from advisory-only to advisory+block' but names only passes.py and orchestrator.py
findings.finding: The `plan_review_coach` workflow op in `workflow_ops.py` (lines 475-524) is also an unenumerated edit target: it reads `surviving = list(ctx.inputs.get('surfaced') or [])` to compute applicable triggers for render — if blocking findings carry different criteria tags, they will be excluded from the trigger set and their applicable move subset will be narrowed incorrectly.
findings.location: What paragraph; src/rebar/llm/plan_review/workflow_ops.py:497-500
scenarios: A blocking finding on a criterion with an `applies_when`-gated move would not trigger that move because its criteria are not in the trigger set — the move is not in the applicable subset, so it can never be picked for that blocking finding.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `src/rebar/llm/reviewers/plan_review_coach.md` and `coach.py:coach_listing` to the edit-target list to update the advisory-scoped language to cover blocking findings when they are included in the input.
evidence: coach.py:123: `'## Surviving advisory findings (by id)\n'` — header says advisory-only
plan_review_coach.md:14: 'For each SURVIVING advisory finding, select…' — prompt explicitly scopes to advisory
Plan says the coach prompt is 'src/rebar/llm/reviewers/plan_review_coach.md' but lists it only as existing context, not as an edit target
findings.finding: The `coach_listing` function in `coach.py` hardcodes the section header `'## Surviving advisory findings (by id)\n'` (line 123). When blocking findings are mixed into the `surviving` list passed to it, the prompt instruction seen by the LLM contradicts the actual content, and the `plan-review-coach.md` prompt itself instructs the LLM to coach 'SURVIVING advisory findings.' The plan does not mention updating either of these strings.
findings.location: What paragraph / src/rebar/llm/review_kernel/coach.py:123; src/rebar/llm/reviewers/plan_review_coach.md:14
scenarios: The LLM receives a coach prompt saying 'For each SURVIVING advisory finding' but the input listing includes blocking findings. The LLM may skip blocking findings because its instructions say they are advisory-only, or be confused by the mismatch — defeating the coaching goal for blocking findings.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly scope the implementation to both seams of the workflow path: (a) `plan_review_coach_inputs` (workflow_ops.py) must include blocking findings in the coach listing passed to the LLM, and (b) `plan_review_coach` must render notes for them — the plan currently names only `passes.py` as the implementation seam.
evidence: Plan: 'Implementation seam: the pass-4 call in src/rebar/llm/plan_review/passes.py (MOVE_REGISTRY lives there; re-exported by orchestrator.py) - widen the finding set it receives from advisory-only to advisory+block'
workflow_ops.py:366: `surviving = list(ctx.inputs.get('surviving') or [])` in plan_review_coach_inputs — this feeds only surfaced (advisory) findings to the coach prompt
workflow_ops.py:497: `surviving = list(ctx.inputs.get('surfaced') or [])` in plan_review_coach — the render step also only draws from surfaced
coach.py:195-216: coach() gates on `if not surviving: return []` — if blocking findings are never in the listing, no notes are picked, nothing is rendered
findings.finding: The plan under-specifies the two-seam nature of the workflow path: widening `pass4_coach` in `passes.py` covers the bespoke (non-workflow) path, but in the workflow path the coach LLM prompt is built by `plan_review_coach_inputs` (workflow_ops.py:360-375), which currently reads only `ctx.inputs.get('surfaced')` (advisory findings). If `plan_review_coach_inputs` is not also widened to include blocking findings in the coach listing, the LLM sees only advisories and produces no raw notes for blocking findings — the render step in `plan_review_coach` then has nothing to render for them, making the feature inert on the live workflow path.
findings.location: What section ('Implementation seam: the pass-4 call in src/rebar/llm/plan_review/passes.py'); also src/rebar/llm/plan_review/workflow_ops.py:360-375 (plan_review_coach_inputs)
scenarios: On the live workflow path (the gate's sole plan-review path since B-RETIRE), a BLOCK verdict gets no coaching notes for its blocking findings because the LLM coach prompt listing never included them — the feature appears to work in the bespoke/unit-test path but is inert in production.
The AC unit test (synthetic verdict with 1 block + 1 advisory) may pass if it tests only the bespoke pass4_coach path but miss the workflow-path seam entirely.
findings.suggested_fix: In the What section, add: 'Also widen `plan_review_coach_inputs` (workflow_ops.py) to pass `blocking + surfaced` as the finding list to the coach prompt, so the LLM actually sees blocking findings when picking moves; and widen `plan_review_coach` to render notes keyed against both blocking and surfaced finding ids.' The AC unit test should cover the workflow-path seam (plan_review_coach_inputs + plan_review_coach) not just the bespoke pass4_coach path.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify how the coach prompt (`plan_review_coach.md`) and the `coach_listing` header string are updated (or parameterised) to correctly address both advisory and blocking findings without a pink-elephant 'advisory-only' framing.
evidence: plan_review_coach.md:14: 'For each SURVIVING advisory finding, select the single most useful MOVE'
coach.py:123: header string '## Surviving advisory findings (by id)' is hardcoded
Plan says 'same move registry, same subject validator, same deterministic rendering' but does not mention updating the prompt or the `coach_listing` header string
ABSENCE: no mention in the plan of updating the coach prompt or coach_listing header to accept both finding types
findings.finding: The coach prompt (`plan_review_coach.md`) explicitly addresses only 'SURVIVING advisory finding[s]' — feeding blocking findings through the same prompt without a prompt update creates an instruction-locality / pink-elephant conflict where the LLM is told to process advisory findings while receiving blocking ones.
findings.location: src/rebar/llm/reviewers/plan_review_coach.md line 14; plan 'What' section, first bullet
scenarios: The LLM sees the instruction 'For each SURVIVING advisory finding' but is presented with blocking findings in the listing — the LLM may skip or misattribute blocking findings based on the 'advisory' framing in the prompt.
The `coach_listing` header '## Surviving advisory findings (by id)' will appear in the prompt even when the list contains blocking findings, creating a misleading label.
findings.suggested_fix: Either add a prompt-update step to the scope (update the framing to 'surviving findings' generically), or parameterise `coach_listing` to accept a label string so blocking and advisory findings can be listed with the correct header.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Extend the plan's implementation scope to cover `plan_review_coach_inputs` and `plan_review_coach` in `workflow_ops.py`, specifying how blocking findings are added to their `surviving`/`surfaced` inputs on the workflow path.
evidence: workflow_ops.py:366: `surviving = list(ctx.inputs.get('surviving') or [])` — reads advisory-only
workflow_ops.py:497: `surviving = list(ctx.inputs.get('surfaced') or [])` — reads advisory cap subset only
Plan says 'Implementation seam: the pass-4 call in src/rebar/llm/plan_review/passes.py' — does not mention workflow_ops.py
ABSENCE: no mention of `plan_review_coach_inputs` or `plan_review_coach` step ops in the plan's scope
findings.finding: The plan's implementation seam is described as 'the pass-4 call in passes.py' but two workflow-path entry points — `plan_review_coach_inputs` (workflow_ops.py:360) and `plan_review_coach` (workflow_ops.py:475) — independently construct the surviving-finding set from advisory-only inputs (`ctx.inputs.get('surviving')` and `ctx.inputs.get('surfaced')` respectively) and are not mentioned in the plan's scope or What section.
findings.location: src/rebar/llm/plan_review/workflow_ops.py lines 360-375 and 475-524; plan 'What' section, first bullet
scenarios: On the production workflow path, blocking findings are never passed to the coach step because `plan_review_coach_inputs` reads only `surviving` (advisory) and `plan_review_coach` reads only `surfaced` (advisory cap) — the passes.py widening has no effect on the live workflow.
The bespoke `pass4_coach` in passes.py is only used on the non-workflow path; the workflow path diverges through workflow_ops.py where the finding-set widening is absent.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm that `workflow_ops.plan_review_coach_inputs` and `workflow_ops.plan_review_coach` are included in the change surface alongside `passes.py`, so the live workflow path also widens to advisory+block findings.
evidence: Plan: 'Implementation seam: the pass-4 call in src/rebar/llm/plan_review/passes.py'
workflow_ops.py:374: `surviving = list(ctx.inputs.get('surviving') or [])` — advisory-only hardcode
workflow_ops.py:497: `surviving = list(ctx.inputs.get('surfaced') or [])` — advisory-only hardcode in render step
ABSENCE: neither workflow_ops step is listed in the plan's change surface
findings.finding: The plan names `passes.py` as the sole implementation seam ('Implementation seam: the pass-4 call in src/rebar/llm/plan_review/passes.py'), but the live workflow path routes through two additional `workflow_ops` steps — `plan_review_coach_inputs` (which hardcodes `surviving = ctx.inputs.get('surfaced')`, advisory-only) and `plan_review_coach` (which reads `ctx.inputs.get('surfaced')` for the trigger set) — that also need widening. Omitting these from the change surface means the workflow path silently stays advisory-only even after `passes.py` is widened.
findings.location: What / implementation seam paragraph; src/rebar/llm/plan_review/workflow_ops.py lines 360-375 (plan_review_coach_inputs) and lines 486-500 (plan_review_coach)
scenarios: Agent runs `rebar review-plan` via the workflow path on a BLOCK verdict; `plan_review_coach_inputs` still builds the coach prompt over advisory-only findings; blocking findings get no coaching note despite the plan's AC1.
findings.suggested_fix: Add `workflow_ops.plan_review_coach_inputs` and `workflow_ops.plan_review_coach` to the 'What' section's implementation seam list, and add an AC oracle that exercises the workflow path (not just the bespoke `pass4_coach`) with a blocking finding.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an acceptance criterion (or extend AC#1) with an explicit oracle asserting that coaching entries for blocking findings carry a `decision` value distinguishable from advisory entries (e.g., `entry['decision'] == 'block'` for the blocking finding in the synthetic verdict).
evidence: What: 'tagging each coaching note with the finding's decision so consumers can distinguish'
AC#1 checks only that 2 coaching entries exist and each carries guide_url — it does not assert the presence or value of the decision tag
No other AC references the decision/distinguishability requirement
findings.finding: The What section describes a 'decision' tag on each coaching note so consumers can distinguish blocking vs advisory coaching entries, but no Acceptance Criterion verifies that the tag is present or correct on blocking-finding coaching entries.
findings.location: What section ('tagging each coaching note with the finding's decision'); Acceptance Criteria section
scenarios: The decision tag is silently omitted in implementation; no test fails because no criterion verifies it, yet the stated distinguishability goal from the Why/What sections is unmet.
findings.suggested_fix: Extend AC#1's oracle: 'the coaching entry for the blocking finding has `decision == "block"` and the advisory entry has `decision == "advisory"` (or equivalent distinguishing field).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify where the `decision` tag is injected onto blocking-finding coaching notes (e.g., post-render in the workflow op or finalize_verdict, NOT inside the shared render_coach_notes kernel), and verify this does not require changing the kernel contract.
evidence: Plan: 'tagging each coaching note with the finding's decision so consumers can distinguish'
coach.py lines 162-185: render_coach_notes outputs {move_id, move_name, subject, finding_refs, coaching} — no decision field
workflow_ops.py:500: coaching = passes.render_coach_notes(list(ctx.inputs.get('notes') or []), applicable) — no decision injection after the call either
findings.finding: The plan asserts 'tagging each coaching note with the finding's decision so consumers can distinguish,' but render_coach_notes() (the sole rendering function the plan says will be reused unchanged) outputs only {move_id, move_name, subject, finding_refs, coaching} — there is no decision parameter or output field. The plan does not specify where the decision tag is injected (inside render_coach_notes, in the workflow op, or in finalize_verdict), leaving the implementation of this assertion unspecified.
findings.location: What section / coach.py:162-185 (render_coach_notes) + plan 'What' bullet 1
scenarios: Implementer follows the plan literally (reuse render_coach_notes unchanged) but the decision tag never appears on coaching notes, so consumers cannot distinguish block vs advisory coaching.
Implementer adds decision to render_coach_notes, contradicting the plan's claim that the mechanism is unchanged ('same deterministic rendering').
findings.suggested_fix: Add a post-render step in the workflow op (plan_review_coach in workflow_ops.py) or in finalize_verdict that stamps note['decision'] = 'block'|'advisory' based on the finding_refs lookup — keeping render_coach_notes byte-identical.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Enumerate the exact edit points for the `decision` field tagging on coaching notes: specify whether `render_coach_notes` is modified, or whether the `plan_review_coach` workflow op post-processes notes to inject `decision` from the finding partition.
evidence: coach.py:162-185: `render_coach_notes` returns notes with keys `{move_id, move_name, subject, finding_refs, coaching}` — no `decision` field
Plan says: 'tagging each coaching note with the finding's decision so consumers can distinguish' — implies a new output field on coaching notes
Plan's OUT list: 'any change to…sidecar schema' — but the coaching note output shape is not the sidecar schema; this is a new field on in-memory notes
findings.finding: The plan states coaching notes should be 'tagged with the finding's decision so consumers can distinguish' blocking from advisory coaching notes, but `render_coach_notes` in `coach.py` does not receive or propagate a `decision` field — it only outputs `{move_id, move_name, subject, finding_refs, coaching}`. Adding a `decision` field to the rendered note is new behavior (a CREATION) not present today.
findings.location: What paragraph ('tagging each coaching note with the finding's decision'); src/rebar/llm/review_kernel/coach.py:162-185
scenarios: Without a `decision` field on the note, a CLI consumer or downstream reader cannot distinguish block coaching from advisory coaching — the stated consumer benefit is not delivered.
findings.suggested_fix: The cleanest approach is to have `plan_review_coach` (workflow_ops.py) post-process the rendered notes to attach `decision` by looking up each note's `finding_refs` against the blocking/surfaced partition — this keeps `render_coach_notes` generic and the kernel unchanged.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the exact placement (kernel `render_coach_notes`, `finalize_verdict`, or workflow op) and vocabulary ('block'/'advisory') for the `decision` field on coaching notes, and specify whether the `_pass4_model` schema or `render_coach_notes` return type is amended.
evidence: Plan: 'tagging each coaching note with the finding's decision so consumers can distinguish'
render_coach_notes output keys: {move_id, move_name, subject, finding_refs, coaching} — no `decision` field
_pass4_model CoachNote schema: {move_id, subject, finding_refs} — no `decision` field
ABSENCE: no specification of where the `decision` field is injected (kernel render_coach_notes vs finalize_verdict vs workflow op) or what values it takes ('block'/'advisory'?)
findings.finding: The plan says coaching notes should be 'tagged with the finding's decision so consumers can distinguish' blocking from advisory coaching entries, but neither the `render_coach_notes` output schema (coach.py), the `_pass4_model` structured-output contract (passes.py:186-201), nor `finalize_verdict` define where or how this `decision` tag is added, and the value vocabulary is not named.
findings.location: plan 'What' section first bullet; src/rebar/llm/review_kernel/coach.py:162-185; src/rebar/llm/plan_review/passes.py:186-201
scenarios: An implementer cannot determine where to add the `decision` field — in the shared kernel `render_coach_notes` (affecting all gates), in plan-review's `finalize_verdict`, or in the workflow op — without additional specification.
Without a defined vocabulary, consumers (CLI renderer, sidecar persistence) cannot reliably branch on the `decision` value to distinguish blocking from advisory coaching notes.
findings.suggested_fix: Since `render_coach_notes` in the shared kernel does not have finding-level decision context, the most natural placement is in `finalize_verdict` (which separates blocking from surfaced) or in the workflow op — document this explicitly in the plan.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm whether `coach_listing`'s 'Surviving advisory findings (by id)' heading needs to be updated (or parameterised) to accurately reflect a mixed advisory+block finding set when the plan's extension is applied.
evidence: Plan: 'same move registry, same subject validator, same deterministic rendering' — implies coach_listing is used unchanged
ABSENCE: the plan does not mention updating the coach_listing heading for the mixed advisory+block case
findings.finding: The kernel `coach_listing` function (which both the bespoke and workflow paths call) emits the fixed heading `'Surviving advisory findings (by id)'` into the LLM prompt. If blocking findings are added to the same listing without updating this label, the coach prompt misrepresents the finding set to the LLM, which may affect pick quality.
findings.location: src/rebar/llm/review_kernel/coach.py lines 116-125 (coach_listing / coach_instructions)
scenarios: The LLM coach receives a prompt saying 'advisory findings' but is asked to pick moves for a blocking finding, creating a semantic mismatch that could reduce pick accuracy for blocking-finding coaching.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Either (a) add 'decision' to the sidecar build_payload coaching pick list (acknowledging the schema extension) or (b) remove the 'decision' tag requirement from coaching notes and instead distinguish block vs advisory coaching via the verdict structure (separate lists), so the 'no schema change' OUT clause is actually true.
evidence: Plan Scope OUT: 'coaching entries for blocking findings flow through the existing coaching[] persistence unchanged (additive; no schema change)'
Plan What: 'tagging each coaching note with the finding's decision so consumers can distinguish'
sidecar.py:445-454: build_payload copies only move_id, move_name, subject, finding_refs, coaching — a new 'decision' key is not picked and would be silently dropped
findings.finding: The plan's OUT-of-scope clause states 'no schema change' for coaching[] persistence, but simultaneously requires each coaching note to carry a new 'decision' field. The sidecar's build_payload (sidecar.py:445-454) copies a fixed field list: {move_id, move_name, subject, finding_refs, coaching}. A 'decision' field on a coaching note is silently dropped by the current sidecar serializer unless the pick list is extended — which IS a schema change. The two claims are contradictory, and the false exclusion hides necessary sidecar work.
findings.location: Scope section ('OUT: … no schema change') + sidecar.py:445-454
scenarios: A downstream sidecar consumer (audit UI, offline calibration tool) that reads coaching[] from the sidecar never sees the decision field because it was dropped at persistence time — the consumer-distinguishability goal is broken in the audit path.
If the decision tag is added to the verdict's coaching[] but not to the sidecar pick list, the sidecar schema and the live verdict shape diverge, creating a silent inconsistency.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve the contradiction between What ('tag coaching notes with decision') and Scope OUT ('no schema change'): either (a) explicitly permit the coaching-entry schema to gain a `decision` field and remove it from Scope OUT, or (b) explain how distinguishability is achieved without a schema change (e.g., a wrapper field only in-memory, not persisted).
evidence: What: 'tagging each coaching note with the finding's decision so consumers can distinguish'
Scope OUT: 'no sidecar schema change'
A new `decision` field on coaching entries is a schema change to the coaching[] payload
findings.finding: The What section says coaching notes should be 'tagged with the finding's decision so consumers can distinguish,' which implies adding a new field (e.g., `decision`) to coaching entry objects. The Scope section simultaneously declares 'OUT: no sidecar schema change.' Adding a decision field to coaching entries IS a schema change, creating a direct contradiction between these two sections.
findings.location: What section ('tagging each coaching note with the finding's decision') vs. Scope section ('no sidecar schema change')
scenarios: Implementer adds the decision tag (following What) and breaks the no-schema-change constraint (Scope), or omits the tag (following Scope) and loses the distinguishability the What section demands — two conflicting directions.
findings.suggested_fix: In Scope, narrow the 'no sidecar schema change' exclusion to mean 'no change to the top-level sidecar envelope or blocking/advisory thresholds,' and explicitly allow the coaching entry object to gain an optional `decision: 'block' | 'advisory'` field, consistent with the additive posture already stated.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve the tension between 'no schema change' and 'tagging each coaching note with the finding's decision': either explicitly declare the `decision` field as a new (additive) sidecar schema field and add a one-line doc/schema note for it, or describe the alternative mechanism by which CLI/consumers distinguish blocking vs advisory coaching entries without a new field.
evidence: 'tagging each coaching note with the finding's decision so consumers can distinguish' (What section)
'coaching[] persistence unchanged (additive; no schema change)' (What section)
No mention of sidecar schema doc, ADR, or CLAUDE.md update anywhere in the plan
findings.finding: The plan simultaneously claims 'no schema change' for the sidecar and introduces a new 'decision' tag on coaching entries so consumers can distinguish blocking vs advisory notes. These two statements are in tension: if 'decision' is a new field on coaching entries, it IS a schema addition; if it is not, consumers have no reliable way to distinguish findings. Neither the sidecar schema docs nor any ADR/CLAUDE.md update is planned to capture this new cross-component contract field, leaving CLI, sidecar, and downstream agent consumers without a documented contract.
findings.location: What section: '…tagging each coaching note with the finding's decision so consumers can distinguish' vs Scope: 'additive; no schema change'
scenarios: An agent or CLI consumer reads the sidecar and cannot reliably filter blocking-finding coaching entries because the 'decision' field is undocumented and its presence/absence is ambiguous.
A future maintainer changing the coaching schema does not know 'decision' was added here because no ADR or doc was updated.
If 'decision' is truly not added (to satisfy the no-schema-change claim), the CLI renderer has no way to show blocking coaching differently, making AC #3 unverifiable.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a sentence under Scope clarifying: 'The `decision` field is an additive, optional coaching-entry field (string: "block" | "advisory"); update the coaching[] schema comment in orchestrator.py and note the addition in CLAUDE.md or a brief schema-changelog comment.' This resolves both the schema-change ambiguity and the documentation gap.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify graceful-degradation behavior for coaching failures on blocking findings: the blocking verdict MUST be preserved and emitted even if coaching generation fails, and failures should be logged (not swallowed).
evidence: Plan says 'additive; no schema change' and 'passthrough-additive field' but never states that a coaching failure must not suppress or alter the blocking decision
ABSENCE: no error-handling or graceful-degradation clause for the widened coach invocation
If coaching throws on a blocking finding, the existing advisory path would be unaffected but the block's coaching entry and guide_url could be silently dropped without the agent ever noticing
findings.finding: The plan does not specify what happens when the extended coach invocation fails (throws or returns no result) for a blocking finding — it is silent on whether the blocking verdict itself is preserved or whether the failure is surfaced vs. silently swallowed.
findings.location: What section — 'Extend the Pass-4 coach invocation to also cover blocking findings'; AC1
scenarios: LLM call for coaching times out mid-pass on a blocking finding — block verdict is emitted but with no coaching note and no guide_url, agent retries without actionable guidance
Subject validator rejects the generated note for a blocking finding — exception propagates and the entire pass-4 output is lost
findings.suggested_fix: Add an AC or implementation note: 'If coach invocation raises for a blocking finding, log the exception, emit the finding with coaching=None/empty, and continue — do not propagate the exception to the caller.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add non-happy-path CLI rendering specs for blocking findings: define the rendered output when guide_url is None/missing and when no move is found in the registry (e.g., omit the link bracket, render a fallback label, or suppress the coaching line gracefully).
evidence: AC3 only asserts 'rendered text contains the guide_url for a blocking finding' — the green path
ABSENCE: no spec for empty/None guide_url case (criterion with no guide entry), no spec for registry-miss (blocking finding whose criterion has no registered move)
Existing advisory renderer has the same '-> <coaching> [<link>]' pattern but its non-happy-path behavior for missing link is not referenced
findings.finding: The CLI rendering spec covers only the happy path (guide_url present, coaching note generated); it does not specify what `rebar review-plan` should render when guide_url is absent/None or when no matching move exists in the registry for a blocking finding.
findings.location: What section — CLI bullet; AC3
scenarios: A blocking finding maps to a criterion that has no entry in docs/plan-review-criteria-guide.md — guide_url is None, CLI either crashes on string formatting or renders a broken '[None]' link
A blocking finding's criterion has no matching move in the MOVE_REGISTRY — rendered note is empty or raises KeyError, and the CLI output is malformed for the BLOCK verdict
findings.suggested_fix: Extend AC3 with a second assertion: 'when guide_url is None the rendered line omits the link bracket entirely (no crash, no "[None]")'; add a registry-miss case that logs a warning and omits the coaching line rather than raising.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Enumerate the downstream consumers of `coaching[]` entries (e.g. CLI renderer, sidecar reader, any report/dashboard) and confirm each tolerates the new `decision` tag field, or explicitly acknowledge them in the Scope/What section.
evidence: "tagging each coaching note with the finding's decision so consumers can distinguish"
"coaching entries for blocking findings flow through the existing coaching[] persistence unchanged (additive; no schema change)"
Plan adds a new field but names no downstream consumers of coaching[] who might need to handle it
findings.finding: The plan adds a `decision` tag field to coaching entries (so consumers can distinguish block vs. advisory) but does not enumerate or acknowledge which downstream consumers currently read coaching[] entries and must be updated or at least verified to tolerate the new field, despite asserting 'no schema change' for the sidecar.
findings.location: What section — 'tagging each coaching note with the finding's decision so consumers can distinguish'; Scope — 'additive; no schema change'
scenarios: A downstream consumer (e.g. a report renderer or dashboard that iterates coaching entries) receives an unexpected `decision` field and either crashes or silently ignores it, masking a bug
A consumer that relied on all coaching entries being advisory-only changes behavior without the plan author realizing
findings.suggested_fix: Add a bullet under 'What' listing known consumers of coaching[] (e.g. CLI renderer in _llm_commands.py, sidecar persistence layer, any downstream readers) and confirm each is additive-safe; if any consumer iterates coaching entries with a fixed schema, add a AC sub-bullet or note that a forward-compat check was done.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a unit-test case (or explicit AC sub-bullet) asserting graceful degradation when a blocking finding's criterion has no matching move in the MOVE_REGISTRY (e.g. omit coaching entry, emit a no-op, or surface a warning — whichever the existing advisory path does).
evidence: AC1 oracle: 'synthetic verdict with 1 block + 1 advisory yields 2 coaching entries, each carrying guide_url'
No AC or test described for a blocking finding with a criterion not present in MOVE_REGISTRY
Plan says 'same move registry' implying reuse of existing behavior, but existing behavior for the advisory case under missing moves is not documented or tested here
findings.finding: The testing plan has no failure-path test for a blocking finding whose criterion ID has no matching entry in the MOVE_REGISTRY — the plan's ACs test only the happy path (move found, note rendered, guide_url attached) and leave unspecified what the coach emits or how it degrades when no move matches a blocking finding.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC1: 'synthetic verdict with 1 block + 1 advisory yields 2 coaching entries'
scenarios: A new blocking criterion is introduced whose move_id is not yet in the registry; the coach silently drops the coaching entry or raises an unhandled exception; the agent sees no coaching note and cannot remediate
The widened finding-set includes criteria that advisories never exercised, revealing a latent missing-move bug only on blocking findings
findings.suggested_fix: Extend AC1's oracle to include a third sub-case: a blocking finding with an unregistered criterion_id produces a predictable outcome (no coaching entry OR a fallback entry without move_id), consistent with how advisory findings handle the same condition.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Reconcile the leaf's runtime coach-invocation widening with the parent ba33's lossless-capture scope: either update ba33 to explicitly include 'extending what Pass-4 generates at runtime' (which stales ba33's plan-review attestation and requires its re-review), or reparent this story under a different epic whose scope covers coach-behavior changes.
evidence: Parent ba33 Success Criteria: 'captures the full four-pass record… pass-4 coaching — across ALL buckets, not just surfaced ones' — 'captures' implies persistence of existing output, not generation of new output

Parent ba33 Scope IN: 'sidecar payload enrichment (plan + code), completion PASS-path capture + agent output-contract change, retention-cap change, the audit read layer' — runtime coach-invocation widening does not appear

The leaf's Why section acknowledges the coach 'currently runs only over SURVIVING ADVISORY findings' — meaning blocking findings produce no coaching output today; the leaf proposes to create that output, not merely capture existing output
Closed sibling a3db ('pass-4 coaching lossless persistence') addressed the capture side; the leaf proposes a distinct runtime extension the parent's contract does not cover
findings.finding: The leaf extends Pass-4's *runtime generation* of coaching to blocking findings (where none previously existed), but the parent epic ba33 is scoped to *lossless capture* of what the review pipeline already produces — not to changing what the pipeline generates at runtime.
findings.location: What section: 'Extend the Pass-4 coach invocation to also cover blocking findings'
scenarios: The parent ba33 is already closed and its completion attestation covers all six child stories; a new story widening the coach's runtime behavior adds a deliverable the parent's plan-review attestation was not evaluated against, potentially invalidating the parent's scope boundary
findings.suggested_fix: If the intent is genuinely to extend the coach's runtime behavior (not merely persist existing output), update ba33's What/Scope to include 'extend Pass-4 coach invocation to blocking findings' and re-submit ba33 for plan-review before claiming this leaf. Alternatively, reparent the leaf under an epic whose scope explicitly covers improvements to the pass-4 coaching UX.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Either remove the `rebar review-plan` CLI rendering deliverable from the leaf's scope (limiting to sidecar persistence only, which IS within parent scope), or update ba33 to move CLI rendering from OUT to IN scope and re-submit ba33 for plan-review before claiming this leaf.
evidence: Parent ba33 Scope OUT: 'any web UI or rendering (the follow-on epic)'
Parent ba33 Scope IN: 'the audit read layer' — implemented by child 46f0 as 'rebar audit show' + MCP, not as changes to the review-time CLI command rebar review-plan
The leaf references src/rebar/_cli/_llm_commands.py and the 'existing -> <coaching> [<link>] renderer' — this is the live review CLI output path, not the audit read layer
findings.finding: The leaf delivers a CLI rendering change to `rebar review-plan` (`src/rebar/_cli/_llm_commands.py`), but the parent ba33 explicitly places 'any web UI or rendering' OUT of scope and limits its read-layer IN scope to `rebar audit show` + MCP (delivered by story 46f0).
findings.location: What section: 'CLI: rebar review-plan renders the coaching line + guide link for blocking findings'; AC3: 'rebar review-plan output for a BLOCK verdict shows the coaching line + guide link'
scenarios: A reviewer evaluating ba33 did not audit changes to rebar review-plan's rendering because the parent scoped that out; the leaf silently adds rendering work the parent's plan-review attestation excluded
findings.suggested_fix: Narrow the leaf to: extend the coach invocation + guide_url attachment + sidecar persistence only (no rendering changes). CLI rendering of blocking-finding coaching can be a separate story under an epic that explicitly includes review-output UX improvements.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
